title: vis project.json
description: Per-project configuration for @visulima/vis. Place a project.json in each workspace package root.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor autocomplete.

projectType.description: Whether this project is a library (consumed by others) or an application (deployment target).

sourceRoot.description: Source root directory, relative to this project's root. Used for display and language inference.

tags.description: Filterable tags. Used by query language (--query "tag=frontend"), scoped task defaults, and tag-relationship constraints.

layer.description: Project layer in the dependency hierarchy. When enforceLayerRelationships is enabled, projects may only depend on the same or lower layer: configuration < library < scaffolding < tool < automation < application.

stack.description: Technology stack classification. Used by query language and scoped task defaults.

language.description: Primary programming language (e.g. "typescript", "rust", "go"). Used by query language and scoped task defaults.

implicitDependencies.description: Workspace project names this project implicitly depends on (not expressed in package.json).

owners.description: Code ownership declarations. Aggregated by `vis sync codeowners` to generate a CODEOWNERS file.

path.description: File or glob pattern relative to this project root (e.g. "src/**", ".").

channel.description: Optional notification channel (e.g. Slack channel). Emitted as a trailing comment in CODEOWNERS.

description.description: Short description of the project's purpose.

targets.description: Named build/test/run targets. Merged on top of package.json scripts — the command from scripts is used as the default if no command is set here.

command.description: The shell command to execute. Overrides the package.json script of the same name.

executor.description: Alternative to command — an executor identifier.

cache.description: Whether this target's results are cacheable. Defaults based on type: build=true, test=true, run=false.

inputs.description: Input patterns for cache invalidation. Supports file globs, @filegroup:<name> references, and structured input definitions.

outputs.description: Output file/directory patterns produced by this target (e.g. "dist/**").

dependsOn.description: Other targets this target depends on. Can be plain target names (same project) or dependency config objects.

parallelism.description: Whether this target supports parallel execution.

type.description: Semantic task type. Affects caching defaults and CI filtering. build/test are cached by default; run is not.

preset.description: Preset that pre-fills a bundle of options. 'server': persistent, no cache, no CI. 'utility': no cache, no CI.

targetOptions.description: Vis-specific target options controlling execution behavior.

persistent.description: Long-running/never-ending process. Scheduled last, never cached.

interactive.description: Serialized with respect to parallel execution; claims stdin for terminal input.

internal.description: Hidden from CLI listings. Can only be invoked as a dependency of another task.

runInCI.description: Controls whether this task runs in CI. true=always, false=never, 'affected'=only when project is affected, 'always'=even if unaffected.

retryCount.description: Number of times to retry on failure.

retryDelay.description: Delay between retries in ms, or "exponential" for 2^attempt * 1000ms.

affectedFiles.description: How to forward affected files to the task. false=don't, 'args'=as command arguments, 'env'=via VIS_AFFECTED_FILES, 'both'=both.

envFile.description: Dotenv file to load before running. Path relative to the project root.

mutex.description: Named mutex. All tasks sharing the same mutex name are serialized.

osType.description: Restrict execution to specific operating systems. Tasks on non-matching OS are skipped.

runFromWorkspaceRoot.description: Execute from the workspace root instead of the project root.

unixShell.description: Shell override for Linux and macOS. Takes precedence over 'shell'.

windowsShell.description: Shell override for Windows. Takes precedence over 'shell'.

target.description: The target name to depend on.

dependencies.description: When true, run this target on all dependency projects first (equivalent to turbo's ^target).

projects.description: Specific project name(s) to run this target on.

params.description: Whether to forward or ignore parameters from the parent task.

env.description: Environment variable name to include in cache key.

runtime.description: Shell command whose output is included in cache key (e.g. "node --version").

externalDependencies.description: npm package names whose resolved versions are included in cache key.
